Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > Have you pushed this upstream to Linus tree?
> 
> I have not pushed this upstream. This is purely Lustre specific changes where for the block comments(multi line comments) we ignore the same line termination warning. While, Upstream flags this as warnings.

I think if we changed block comment handling, we should disallow 'net' style block comments in Lustre:
 /* This is a comment
  * that I don't like
  * the style of
  */
Lustre should use only:
 /*
  * This is a comment
  * that I like the style
  * of
  */
I think we should still disallow:
 /* This is a comment
  * that I don't like the
  * style of */
since Linux doesn't like it.

https://www.kernel.org/doc/html/v4.10/process/coding-style.html#commenting
https://wiki.lustre.org/Lustre_Coding_Style_Guidelines#Function_comment_blocks

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 2:
> > > 
> > > Have you pushed this upstream to Linus tree?
> > 
> > I have not pushed this upstream. This is purely Lustre specific changes where for the block comments(multi line comments) we ignore the same line termination warning. While, Upstream flags this as warnings.
> 
> I think if we changed block comment handling, we should disallow 'net' style block comments in Lustre:
>  /* This is a comment
>   * that I don't like
>   * the style of
>   */
> Lustre should use only:
>  /*
>   * This is a comment
>   * that I like the style
>   * of
>   */
> I think we should still disallow:
>  /* This is a comment
>   * that I don't like the
>   * style of */
> since Linux doesn't like it.
> 

The present checkpatch.pl handles this what you have mentioned above.
The change in this patch is to _not_ complain on style as below. This 
is disallowed and not liked by Linux, however this style does _save_ a line when we are not
forced to put the terminating comment marker in a new line.

 I think we should still disallow:
  /* This is a comment
   * that I don't like the
   * style of */

On the other hand, the current patch does complain on styles as below to discourage putting terminating marker in new line.

  /* This is a comment
   * that I don't like
   * the style of
   */

and...

  /*
   * This is a comment
   * that I like the style
   * of
   */

> https://www.kernel.org/doc/html/v4.10/process/coding-style.html#commenting
> https://wiki.lustre.org/Lustre_Coding_Style_Guidelines#Function_comment_blocks

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

I hate to see Lustre's checkpatch divert to much from the one upstream.
Please push this change first upstream so we can then apply the final version.

P.S A nice update would be to give warnings when Lustre kernel code uses __uXX values since those are mean only for user land interfaces. Developers tend to use the user land values to this day but its incorrect.

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> I hate to see Lustre's checkpatch divert to much from the one upstream.
> Please push this change first upstream so we can then apply the final version.

This is very Lustre specific. Not sure this should be upstream. checkpatch.pl presently does this correctly by flagging the warnings. Running checkpatch on old code it shows warning if the multi-line comments is not on separate line. This patch suppress that warning it makes looking the at checkpatch output little easier.

In a way it goes "little" against the kernel style standard and for these reasons I do not think this should be upstream. Most likely I will abandon this.

> 
> P.S A nice update would be to give warnings when Lustre kernel code uses __uXX values since those are mean only for user land interfaces. Developers tend to use the user land values to this day but its incorrect.

Done. LU-16774 created.

Thanks

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> (1 comment)
Abandoned

parents.subject: LU-16732 ldiskfs: update for ext4-delayed-iput for RHEL9.1

commit.subject: LU-16730 build: Change block comment handling
commit.message:
LU-16730 build: Change block comment handling

This patch changes checkpatch handling for block
comments. Such that the start of comment '/*' and
end of comment '*/' if on separate lines will
throw an warning. Additionally it will not throw
and warning if they are on same comment line as it
used to do earlier.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
Change-Id: I01fd9cb75593a149d8f8c60418bd1b6effebe43d
